file.content:
# bujb_privileged_ssh COMMAND... -- run an arbitrary command on the BURN
# node as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER under key-only auth. Pass-through: argv is
# handed to ssh as the remote command without shell wrapping (operator
# prepends `powershell -Command`, `bash -c`, etc. as the platform requires).
# Caller must have invoked bujb_resolve_investiture beforehand. Returns
# ssh's exit code.
bujb_privileged_ssh() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
    || buc_die "bujb_privileged_ssh: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"
  test $# -ge 1 \
    || buc_die "bujb_privileged_ssh: command required"

  buc_step "Privileged SSH: ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST} (${BUZ_FOLIO})"

  local z_exit=0
  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"            \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
      -o ConnectTimeout=15                        \
      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}"      \
      "$@"                                        \
    || z_exit=$?
  return "${z_exit}"
}

# eof

content.text:
Tracing the difference between the preflight (which works) and a direct `buw-jpS` invocation of the same command (which produces no output):

| Path | Connect | Remote command | stdout handling | Result |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Preflight (line 554) | ssh direct | `powershell ... wsl.exe --list --quiet` | redirected to file | reads ✓ distros |
| `buw-jpS` (line 917) | ssh `"$@"` pass-through | `wsl.exe --list --verbose` (no shell) | flows to caller terminal | empty |
| `buw-jpS` + powershell wrap | ssh `"$@"` pass-through | `powershell ... wsl.exe --list --verbose \| Out-String` | flows to caller terminal | empty |

Mechanism: `buw-jpS` lands the remote command in cmd.exe (Windows OpenSSH default shell). The user's `ls`/`pwd` tests confirm this — cmd.exe rejected them. The buw-jpS docstring explicitly says: *"operator prepends `powershell -Command`, `bash -c`, etc. as the platform requires."*

The diagnostic-emitted hints currently emit raw `wsl.exe ...` which lands in cmd.exe. wsl.exe over non-interactive SSH+cmd.exe doesn't reliably emit to stdout regardless of `--quiet`/`--verbose` (Windows console-handle quirk; the preflight only works because we control the exact incantation and capture-to-file path).

The diagnostic body's copy-paste lines, as written, won't give the operator visible output when pasted. They're shape-correct per the docket ("`tt/buw-jpS <investiture> '…'` shape") but not operationally useful end-to-end.

**Repair to attempt:** rewrite the three hints to wrap in `powershell -NoProfile -Command` per the buw-jpS contract, with `[Console]::OutputEncoding` set, matching the preflight's known-working incantation:

```
tt/buw-jpS.PrivilegedSsh.sh bujn-winpc 'powershell -NoProfile -Command "[Console]::OutputEncoding=[System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8; wsl.exe --list --verbose"'
```

Even so, my live test of this exact form against bujn-winpc came back empty — meaning the wsl.exe→ssh-stdout path is unreliable for wsl.exe operations even with PowerShell wrap, in non-redirected-output flow.

I'm not certain the powershell-wrap repair is sufficient to make the operator paste actually work. Possible directions: (a) accept docket as shape-only and ship; (b) extend `buw-jpS` to wrap on Windows; (c) emit hints that capture wsl.exe to a file and `type` it back; (d) something else.

Surfacing rather than guessing — what direction?

content.text:
The user's quoting hypothesis isn't quite the whole story but is close — let me confirm by checking the preflight's actual capture file. xxd of the preflight stdout (above) shows **UTF-16LE** bytes (`70 00 6f 00 64 00 ...` = `p.o.d...`). The preflight's redirect-to-file path captures the UTF-16 bytes intact. The bash logic then "matches" by accident — the case never finds the ASCII `rbtww-main` in the UTF-16 stream, so it always falls through to `buc_die`, and the terminal *displays* the UTF-16 bytes with NULs hidden as zero-width.

So the `[Console]::OutputEncoding=[System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8` in the preflight isn't actually doing what I thought — it's not converting wsl.exe's UTF-16 output. The match-by-fall-through happens to print the right thing visually but the data is binary-corrupt.

Let me verify the launcher is the truncation point for buw-jpS by running ssh outside the launcher:

content.text:
The full root cause is clearer now: PowerShell's `[Console]::OutputEncoding` doesn't actually transcode wsl.exe's output — wsl.exe emits UTF-16LE regardless. The launcher's pipe machinery truncates at NUL, but direct ssh-to-file captures the full UTF-16. The preflight "succeeds" only because the terminal happens to render UTF-16 bytes with NULs invisible.

There's a much simpler fix: wsl.exe respects the `WSL_UTF8` environment variable. Setting it to 1 makes wsl.exe emit UTF-8 directly.

input.command: ssh -i ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_winpc-admin -o IdentitiesOnly=yes -o BatchMode=yes -o ConnectTimeout=15 bhyslop@rocket 'cmd /c "set WSL_UTF8=1 && wsl.exe --list --quiet"' > /tmp/utf8.bin 2> /tmp/utf8.err; echo "exit=$?"; echo "stdout (xxd):"; xxd /tmp/utf8.bin | head -8; echo "stdout (text):"; cat /tmp/utf8.bin

ssh -i ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_winpc-admin -o IdentitiesOnly=yes -o BatchMode=yes -o ConnectTimeout=15 bhyslop@rocket 'powershell -NoProfile -Command "$env:WSL_UTF8=1; wsl.exe --list --quiet"' > /tmp/psutf8.bin 2> /tmp/psutf8.err; echo "exit=$?"; xxd /tmp/psutf8.bin | head -8; echo "----"; cat /tmp/psutf8.bin

content.text:
**Two real bugs identified:**

1. The preflight currently "works" by accident — its `[Console]::OutputEncoding=UTF8` doesn't actually transcode wsl.exe; the file captures UTF-16LE bytes; the case-match against ASCII `rbtww-main` never matches the UTF-16 bytes; so the preflight ALWAYS falls through to the die path and always prints "rbtww-main not installed" — even if it IS installed. The terminal just renders UTF-16 with NULs invisible, so it looks fine cosmetically.

2. The diagnostic hints emit raw `wsl.exe` commands that the operator can paste, but they don't produce useful output through `buw-jpS`'s launcher pipes.

`WSL_UTF8=1` set in PowerShell before wsl.exe fixes both. Two edits:

params.intent:
Wire w-preflight before step 1 via case-dispatch (BCG line 1290: test-and-&&-function relies on set -e exemption that propagates through the entire call tree — replaced with case statement matching the file's established shell-letter dispatch convention). Previously preflight ran *after* step 1, so step 1's wsl.exe-wrapped admin exec failed first with opaque 'Admin SSH failed (exit 255)' before the absent-distro diagnostic could surface.

Preflight PowerShell wrapper switched from [Console]::OutputEncoding=[System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8 to $env:WSL_UTF8=1. The former does not actually transcode wsl.exe's output — wsl.exe emits UTF-16LE bytes regardless, and the captured file contained UTF-16. Bash's $(<file) preserves embedded NULs in bash 3.2, so z_output held UTF-16 bytes; the case-match against ASCII 'rbtww-main' could never match those bytes, so preflight ALWAYS fell through to buc_die — even if rbtww-main were installed. The terminal-rendered output 'looked' clean because NULs render as zero-width. WSL_UTF8=1 is the wsl.exe-supported environment variable that makes wsl.exe genuinely emit UTF-8; preflight's case-match now functions correctly in both presence and absence cases.

Diagnostic hints rewrapped in powershell -NoProfile -Command with embedded $env:WSL_UTF8=1 so operator copy-paste produces readable output via buw-jpS. Previously raw 'wsl.exe --list --verbose' through buw-jpS landed in cmd.exe and surfaced only the first byte 'p' through the launcher's NUL-truncating pipes. Inner double-quotes properly escaped in the buc_die multiline string ($ also escaped to defer expansion).

Verified live against bujn-winpc: preflight surfaces absent-distro diagnostic with three actual distros listed (podman-rbw-vm-ignite, docker-desktop, podman-rbw-vm-deploy) and three copy-paste-ready tt/buw-jpS lines. Emitted first hint copy-pasted produces tabular wsl --list --verbose output through buw-jpS. bash -n green; BCG-compliant (case dispatch over test-&&; no evicted commands; pure parameter expansion for \r strip).

content.text:
Notched as `77986b59`.

**On PowerShell `;` discipline:**

Yes, PowerShell's `;` behaves exactly like bash's — both statements run regardless of the first's outcome. So in principle, the BCG concern applies. But the practical risk profile differs from bash:

- `$env:WSL_UTF8=1` is a **property assignment**, not a command. It dispatches to `[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable` under the hood. It's essentially infallible — a non-empty string assignment to a process-scoped env var has no plausible failure mode short of PowerShell itself crashing (which would also kill the second statement).
- The exit status that SSH propagates is the **last** statement's exit — i.e., `wsl.exe`'s. So if `wsl.exe` fails, the bash side catches it via the existing `|| z_exit=$?` path. Real failures propagate.
- The risk we'd be guarding against: `$env:WSL_UTF8=1` silently fails → `wsl.exe` runs without UTF-8 → emits UTF-16LE → bash captures NUL-laden bytes → case-match against ASCII `rbtww-main` always fails → preflight always reports absent. That's exactly the bug we just fixed; a silent regression to it would be invisible.

**Principled hardening** (belt-and-suspenders for that silent-regression case):

```
'powershell -NoProfile -Command "$ErrorActionPreference=''Stop''; $env:WSL_UTF8=1; wsl.exe --list --quiet"'
```

`$ErrorActionPreference='Stop'` makes any PowerShell-side non-terminating error terminate. It doesn't help with native command exit codes (separate concern handled by `$LASTEXITCODE`), but it catches the edge case where the env assignment hits a runtime error.

**Honest take:** The risk of `$env:WSL_UTF8=1` silently failing is essentially nil. Adding `$ErrorActionPreference='Stop'` is principled discipline but provides no practical safety here. If you want symmetry with the bash discipline, add it; if you want to keep the line tight, the current form is genuinely safe for this case. Your call — both are defensible.

Lets work out how to make it easier to do it right each time.  Maybe a XXX_powershell_{prefix,postfix} variable set where the first one is the `'powershell -NoProfile -Command "$ErrorActionPreference=''Stop''; $env:WSL_UTF8=1;` and the last one is `'` such that whenever you use this, it is really clear?  If that seems convoluted or confusing, I'm okay with a bash function that hides that powershell construct, maybe issues the ssh too.  Thoughts?

content.text:
Bash function strongly preferred. Three reasons:

1. **Structural enforcement.** With prefix/postfix constants, an LLM (me) can forget the postfix, skip the prefix, or paste the wrong incantation between them — exactly the behavior you don't trust. With a function, calling it = discipline applied; not calling it = obviously not using the helper. Binary choice, no middle ground to fail in.

2. **Quoting centralized.** PowerShell-inside-SSH-inside-bash quoting is exactly the minefield where I just tripped twice (lost `"`s, then lost `$`). One helper, one tested-correct quoting recipe, every call site inherits it. Constants don't help with quoting because you still have to glue strings together at the call site.

3. **The pattern already exists.** `zbujb_admin_exec` (line 286) is the precise sibling: shell-letter-aware admin-SSH helper that takes a script body via stdin and wraps in `bash -s` / `wsl.exe ... bash -s`. The PowerShell counterpart slots in naturally.

Sketch:

```bash
zbujb_admin_powershell() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  {
    printf '%s\n' "\$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'"
    printf '%s\n' "\$env:WSL_UTF8 = 1"
    cat
  } | ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}" \
          -o IdentitiesOnly=yes ... \
          "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}" \
          'powershell -NoProfile -Command -'
}
```

Caller writes only the body:

```bash
zbujb_admin_powershell <<'PWSH' > "${stdout_file}" 2> "${stderr_file}"
wsl.exe --list --quiet
PWSH
```

Discipline is structurally inside the helper; caller can't skip it or get the quoting wrong.

**Scope question:** This is a new helper function and replaces two call sites in this file (the preflight invocation and arguably the diagnostic-hint shape, though those are operator-paste and live as text). The current pace (₢A-AAv) is about the WSL user-model correction; introducing a discipline helper is a structurally separate piece of work. I'd suggest slating it as a follow-up pace ("introduce-bujb-admin-powershell-helper" or similar) and wrapping ₢A-AAv as it stands. Or if you want it folded in, I can extend ₢A-AAv's docket via reslate. Your call.

file.content:
## Prohibited Constructs

### ❌ HERE Documents (Heredocs)

```bash
# Never use - breaks indentation
cat <<EOF > "${z_file}"
content here
must be unindented
EOF

# Use temp files or echo sequences instead
echo "content here" > "${z_file}"
echo "stays indented" >> "${z_file}"
```

### ❌ Bash 4+ Features

```bash
# Avoid these (bash 3.2 compatibility)
declare -A z_array              # Associative arrays
z_files=(**/*.txt)              # ** globbing
readarray -t z_lines            # readarray/mapfile
```

### ❌ Bare File Truncation

```bash
# ❌ Looks like a line where the command was accidentally deleted
> "${z_file}"

# ✅ Explicit no-op makes truncation intent unambiguous
: > "${z_file}"
```

Bare `> file` without a command is valid bash but reads like an accident at 3am. The `: >` form explicitly signals "I am creating or truncating this file." Shellcheck enforces this via SC2188.

**Prefer immutable temp files.** BCG temp files are forensic artifacts — preserved for post-mortem debugging. If you find yourself truncating a temp file, the design likely needs a unique filename per step instead of reusing and clearing. Truncation is appropriate only for log files and other non-forensic outputs.

### ❌ Inline Shellcheck Directives

```bash
# ❌ Never suppress shellcheck inline
# shellcheck disable=SC2086
some_command ${z_unquoted}

# ❌ Never use source path hints
# shellcheck source=/dev/null
source "${z_file}"
```

575	Readonly enforcement makes mutation bugs loud at the point of violation rather than silent cascades downstream. Three patterns at different scopes:
576	
577	#### `local -r` — Default for Local Variables
578	
579	Use `local -r` for every local variable that is assigned once and never modified. This is the majority of locals in a typical BCG function.
580

583	local -r z_filepath="${1:-}"

### String Usage

```bash
# Single-line HERE-string allowed for piping variables
jq '.field' <<<"${z_json_data}"
grep "pattern" <<<"${z_content}"

# Multi-line HERE-strings and HERE-docs prohibited
```

### Readonly Patterns

Readonly enforcement makes mutation bugs loud at the point of violation rather than silent cascades downstream. Three patterns at different scopes:

#### `local -r` — Default for Local Variables

Use `local -r` for every local variable that is assigned once and never modified. This is the majority of locals in a typical BCG function.

```bash
# ✅ Default: readonly locals
local -r z_filepath="${1:-}"
local -r z_varname="${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_idx]}"
local -r z_full_ref="${z_image}@${z_digest}"
local -r z_content=$(<"${z_file}")    # $(<file) is a builtin — safe on one line
```

**Four exceptions** where `local` (mutable) is required:

```bash
# Exception 1: Loop counters
local z_index=0
z_index=$((z_index + 1))

content.text:
Designing the helper to mirror `zbujb_admin_exec`'s shape and BCG patterns. Three key compliance choices:

- **Body as positional args**, joined with `$*` — avoids stdin-pumping (which would need `cat`, off-allowlist) and avoids heredocs (BCG line 1690 prohibits)
- **Trailing `$LASTEXITCODE` check** — best-effort native-command exit propagation. PowerShell's `$ErrorActionPreference='Stop'` doesn't catch native commands; the if-check propagates the last command's exit (catches the trailing-statement-masks-failure case)
- **No `|| buc_die` on ssh** — mirrors `zbujb_admin_exec` (line 286): thin transport function, callers handle exit code

file.content:
# zbujb_admin_exec LETTER -- read a bash script from stdin and execute it on
# the remote node as the privileged user, wrapped in the shell-letter-
# appropriate invocation (native bash for b; Cygwin's bash --login for c;
# wsl.exe to BUJB_wsl_distribution as root for w).
zbujb_admin_exec() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  zbujb_assert_shell_letter "${z_letter}"

  local z_remote_invoker
  case "${z_letter}" in
    b) z_remote_invoker='bash -s' ;;
    c) z_remote_invoker='C:/cygwin64/bin/bash --login -s' ;;
    w) z_remote_invoker="wsl.exe --distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} --user root bash -s" ;;
  esac

  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"     \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                         \
      -o BatchMode=yes                              \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new           \
      -o ConnectTimeout=15                          \
      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}" \
      "${z_remote_invoker}"
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Garrison steps (6-step ceremony per BUSJG{B,C,W})

ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"     \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                         \
      -o BatchMode=yes                              \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new           \
      -o ConnectTimeout=15                          \
      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}" \
      "${z_remote_invoker}"
}

# zbujb_admin_powershell BODY... -- run a PowerShell statement chain on
# the remote node as the privileged user. Prepends discipline prelude:
# $ErrorActionPreference='Stop' (PS errors terminate), $env:WSL_UTF8=1
# (wsl.exe emits UTF-8). Trailing if-check propagates $LASTEXITCODE on
# non-zero. Caller redirects stdout/stderr; returns ssh's exit code.
zbujb_admin_powershell() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test $# -ge 1 \
    || buc_die "zbujb_admin_powershell: PowerShell command body required"
  local -r z_body="$*"

  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"            \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
      -o BatchMode=yes                             \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new          \
      -o ConnectTimeout=15                         \
      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}"       \
      "powershell -NoProfile -Command \"\$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'; \$env:WSL_UTF8 = 1; ${z_body}; if (\$LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { exit \$LASTEXITCODE }\""
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Garrison steps (6-step ceremony per BUSJG{B,C,W})

zbujb_admin_powershell() {
  zbujb_sentinel

content.text:
Test passes. Diagnostic output is identical to before the refactor — clean ASCII distros listed, three copy-paste hints emitted with proper escaping. The helper is now in place and the preflight call site reduced from 9 lines of ssh boilerplate to 4 lines.

**Comments on the design:**

- **Discipline structurally enforced.** Every PowerShell-over-SSH call in this module routes through `zbujb_admin_powershell`. There's no path that skips `$ErrorActionPreference='Stop'`, `$env:WSL_UTF8=1`, or the `$LASTEXITCODE` propagation check. An LLM (me) writing a future caller can't accidentally drop the prelude — the function signature is "give me a body, I handle the rest."

- **Quoting centralized.** The function holds the one tested-correct quoting recipe: bash double-quotes around the whole arg, `\"` for the inner PowerShell `-Command "..."` boundary, `\$` for the literal PowerShell variables. Call sites pass the body as a plain string with no quoting concerns.

- **BCG-compliant.** Argument validation via `test $# -ge 1 || buc_die`. `local -r` for the joined body. No external commands (no `cat`, no `tr`). No heredocs. Mirrors `zbujb_admin_exec`'s thin-transport shape (returns ssh's exit code).

- **One known limitation, documented in the comment.** PowerShell's `$ErrorActionPreference='Stop'` doesn't catch native command exit codes (a Windows PowerShell quirk, not a helper deficiency). The trailing `$LASTEXITCODE` check propagates the **last** native command's exit; intermediate failures in multi-command bodies still need per-command checks from the caller. For our preflight (single command), this is moot.

Ready for the second notch when you say.

params.intent:
Introduce zbujb_admin_powershell helper to structurally enforce PowerShell-over-SSH error-trap discipline for privileged admin operations on Windows nodes. Mirrors zbujb_admin_exec's thin-transport shape (sibling function for the PowerShell variant of the bash invokers).

Discipline applied by the helper, not by callers:
- $ErrorActionPreference='Stop' so PowerShell non-terminating errors become terminating and propagate exit codes.
- $env:WSL_UTF8=1 so wsl.exe emits UTF-8 instead of UTF-16LE (transit-clean through bash's NUL-truncating capture).
- Trailing 'if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { exit $LASTEXITCODE }' for best-effort native-command exit propagation (catches the trailing-statement-masks-failure case; multi-command bodies still need per-command checks per the function comment).

Callers pass the body as positional arguments; the helper joins with $* and constructs the full powershell -NoProfile -Command line with prelude prepended. Caller redirects stdout/stderr; helper returns ssh's exit code. Avoids heredocs (BCG line 1690 prohibits) and stdin-pumping (would require cat which is off the POSIX allowlist).

zbujb_garrison_w_preflight refactored to call zbujb_admin_powershell 'wsl.exe --list --quiet' instead of inlining the ssh+powershell boilerplate — reduces preflight's ssh-boilerplate footprint from 9 lines to 4 and routes through the disciplined helper.

BCG compliance: test $# -ge 1 || buc_die (no test-and-&&); local -r for joined body; case-style sentinel call; no external commands; mirrors existing zbujb_admin_exec thin-transport pattern (returns ssh exit code without || buc_die since the function is a transport delegating exit-code handling to caller).

Verified live against bujn-winpc: garrison-WSL surfaces absent-distro diagnostic identically to pre-refactor — three actual distros listed, three copy-paste tt/buw-jpS hints with proper escaping. bash -n green. The diagnostic-emitted hints continue to inline the powershell prelude as text (they're operator-paste, not internal callers); future work could add a tt/buw-jpsW (PowerShell-WSL_UTF8 privileged) tabtarget but that is out of pace scope.

So Ubuntu 24.04 LTS download + install completed in ~31s end-to-end through the helper. Stdout streamed cleanly back through buw-jpS (Downloading → Installing → "Distribution successfully installed"). The `$LASTEXITCODE` propagation worked — wsl.exe returned 0 and that reached the bash side as exit 0.

Worth noting: the install registered `Ubuntu-24.04` as the distro name, not `rbtww-main`. So the preflight will still report `rbtww-main` absent until the operator does an export+import to land it under the canonical name (the third hint's `--import` shape, with a rootfs.tar produced via `wsl.exe --export Ubuntu-24.04 C:\WSL\rbtww-main.tar`).

content.text:
Yes — malformation in the third hint. The `<rootfs.tar>` placeholder uses angle brackets, which PowerShell's parser treats as redirection operators (and `<` is specifically "reserved for future use" per the error). The placeholder is a documentation convention, but when the line is pasted as a real command, PowerShell tries to parse it.

The other two hints (`--list --verbose`, `--install --no-launch`) parse cleanly because they have no angle brackets.

The bash source is at line 586:
```
tt/buw-jpS.PrivilegedSsh.sh ${BUZ_FOLIO} 'powershell -NoProfile -Command \"\$env:WSL_UTF8=1; wsl.exe --import ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} C:\\WSL\\${BUJB_wsl_distribution} <rootfs.tar>\"'
```

The `<rootfs.tar>` reaches PowerShell verbatim. Two reasonable fixes:

**Option A — concrete-looking path** (operator can paste as-is or substitute):
```
wsl.exe --import rbtww-main C:\WSL\rbtww-main C:\WSL\rbtww-main.tar
```
Parseable. Implies a reasonable default location for the .tar. Slightly mis-leading (the .tar must actually exist there, which it won't unless the operator did an export first).

**Option B — non-bracketed placeholder** (visually obvious it's a fill-in):
```
wsl.exe --import rbtww-main C:\WSL\rbtww-main PATH_TO_ROOTFS_TAR
```
Uppercase signals placeholder; PowerShell parses it as an unquoted positional argument (which wsl.exe would then complain about, but the parser doesn't choke).

I'd lean Option A — the path `C:\WSL\rbtww-main.tar` matches the install location and is the natural place to put the .tar. It implies the missing intermediate step (operator exports an installed distro to that path first), which is honest about the workflow without bloating the hint into a multi-line procedure.

Which?

Real failure, not malformation this time — the `.tar` doesn't exist because nothing produced it. The hint sequence as written is incomplete: install creates `Ubuntu-24.04`, but to get `rbtww-main` the operator needs an **export** step in the middle to produce the `.tar` from the installed Ubuntu, then import under the canonical name.

Current sequence (3 lines, broken in the middle):
```
1. wsl.exe --list --verbose
2. wsl.exe --install --no-launch -d Ubuntu-24.04
3. wsl.exe --import rbtww-main C:\WSL\rbtww-main C:\WSL\rbtww-main.tar  ← .tar doesn't exist yet
```

Working sequence (4 lines, with export inserted):
```
1. wsl.exe --list --verbose
2. wsl.exe --install --no-launch -d Ubuntu-24.04
3. wsl.exe --export Ubuntu-24.04 C:\WSL\rbtww-main.tar       ← NEW: produces the .tar
4. wsl.exe --import rbtww-main C:\WSL\rbtww-main C:\WSL\rbtww-main.tar
```

Optional 5th line if you want to clean up the intermediate distro:
```
5. wsl.exe --unregister Ubuntu-24.04
```

Add the export step (and optionally the unregister)?

content.text:
**Diagnosis:** Not malformed — real failure. `wsl.exe --export` doesn't auto-create the parent directory. `C:\WSL\` doesn't exist on bujn-winpc, so when wsl.exe tries to open `C:\WSL\rbtww-main.tar` for writing, the OS reports "path not found" on the parent directory. The error is honest but operator-hostile.

**Two patch-the-hints fixes possible:**
- Prepend `New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path C:\WSL -Force | Out-Null;` to the export line (idempotent — won't fail if dir exists)
- Switch the install location to `$env:LOCALAPPDATA\WSL\${BUJB_wsl_distribution}` which Microsoft Store-style WSL distros use — guaranteed to exist by default

**On tabtarget conversion: yes, you're right.** The five-line paste sequence has real UX problems beyond the missing-directory bug:

- Step 2 (install) isn't idempotent — pasting after a prior install fails with `ERROR_ALREADY_EXISTS` (we just saw that)
- Step 3 (export) needs the C:\WSL dir to exist
- Step 4 (import) needs step 3 to have produced the tar
- Step 5 (unregister) leaves the host in a half-state if step 4 didn't actually land

A tabtarget — call it `tt/buw-jpwI.WslImport.sh <investiture>` or similar — could:
- Use `zbujb_admin_powershell` consistently (the new helper already provides the discipline)
- Pre-flight: skip install if Ubuntu-24.04 already exists; skip export if the .tar already exists; skip if `${BUJB_wsl_distribution}` is already imported
- Pre-create `C:\WSL` (or use `$env:LOCALAPPDATA\WSL`)
- One paste, idempotent, error messages framed for the operator

**Scope question:** That's a structurally separate pace from "correct-wsl-user-model" — new colophon (`buw-jpwI`), new public function in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`, BUSJGW or sibling spec entry, BCG-compliant idempotency checks. Three options:

1. **Fold into ₢A-AAv via reslate** — add the tabtarget as part of this pace. Doable but inflates the scope considerably.
2. **Slate a new pace** ("introduce-wsl-import-tabtarget" or similar) and wrap ₢A-AAv as it stands. The current diagnostic's broken third hint is acknowledged as transitional; the new pace supersedes it.
3. **Patch the export line in this pace** (one-liner: prepend `New-Item ...`), accept the multi-paste UX friction as good-enough, and slate the tabtarget as a deferred itch.

I'd lean (2) — keep the current pace tight, slate a follow-up. The tabtarget is meaningful work and shouldn't ride along on a code-correction pace. Your call.

# bujb_fenestrate - fenestrate ceremony (Windows OpenSSH only).
bujb_fenestrate_command() {
  buc_doc_brief "Fenestrate — admin SSH key trust + sshd_config harden (Windows)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_fenestrate
}

# bujb_privileged_ssh_command - run an arbitrary command as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER.
# Pass-through: argv joined into ssh's remote-command position; no shell wrapping.
bujb_privileged_ssh_command() {
  buc_doc_brief "Privileged SSH — run a command on a node as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER"
  buc_doc_param "command..." "Command tokens executed on the remote node (pass-through)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  test $# -ge 1            || buc_usage_die

  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_privileged_ssh "$@"
}

# bujb_garrison_bash - garrison ceremony, native bash workload shell (Linux/Mac).
bujb_garrison_bash() {
  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_linux/bubep_mac node (shell-letter b)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_garrison b
}

# bujb_garrison_cygwin - garrison ceremony, Cygwin bash workload shell (Windows).
bujb_garrison_cygwin() {
  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_windows node via Cygwin (shell-letter c)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_garrison c
}

# bujb_garrison_wsl - garrison ceremony, WSL bash workload shell (Windows).
bujb_garrison_wsl() {
  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_windows node via WSL (shell-letter w)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_garrison w
}

# bujb_interactive_session - hand control to ssh as workload.
bujb_interactive_session() {
  buc_doc_brief "Interactive SSH session as workload"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

# (the running sshd's old config still permits pubkey auth and now reads
# the updated authorized_keys), so no further prompt. Phase 2 is key-only.
# No ControlMaster, no traps, no 2>/dev/null.

zbujb_fenestrate_assert_platform() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${BURN_PLATFORM}" = "bubep_windows" \
    || buc_die "fenestrate requires bubep_windows, got '${BURN_PLATFORM}'"
}

# zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback STDOUT_FILE STDERR_FILE
# Reads a PowerShell script from stdin and runs it on the remote node as
# the privileged user. publickey,password preferred (BatchMode=no allows
# /dev/tty password prompt on first run). Default Windows OpenSSH shell is
# cmd.exe; we explicitly invoke `powershell -NoProfile -File -` to feed
# the script via stdin. Returns ssh's exit code (caller decides).
zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  local -r z_stdout_file="${1:-}"
  local -r z_stderr_file="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_stdout_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback: stdout_file required"
  test -n "${z_stderr_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback: stderr_file required"

  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"            \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                                \
      -o BatchMode=no                                      \
      -o PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password       \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new                  \
      -o ConnectTimeout=15                                 \
      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}" \

911	# bujb_privileged_ssh COMMAND... -- run an arbitrary command on the BURN
912	# node as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER under key-only auth. Pass-through: argv is
913	# handed to ssh as the remote command without shell wrapping (operator
914	# prepends `powershell -Command`, `bash -c`, etc. as the platform requires).
915	# Caller must have invoked bujb_resolve_investiture beforehand. Returns
916	# ssh's exit code.
917	bujb_privileged_ssh() {
918	  zbujb_sentinel
919	  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
920	    || buc_die "bujb_privileged_ssh: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"
921	  test $# -ge 1 \
922	    || buc_die "bujb_privileged_ssh: command required"

######################################################################
# Public: Fenestrate ceremony

# bujb_fenestrate -- run the two-phase fenestrate ceremony for a Windows
# OpenSSH node. Caller must have invoked bujb_resolve_investiture beforehand.
bujb_fenestrate() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
    || buc_die "bujb_fenestrate: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"

  zbujb_fenestrate_assert_platform

  buc_step "Fenestrate: ${BUZ_FOLIO} (${BURN_HOST})"

  zbujb_fenestrate_phase1
  zbujb_fenestrate_phase2

  buc_step "Fenestrate succeeded"
}

######################################################################
# Public: Privileged SSH (thin admin-side pass-through)

# bujb_privileged_ssh COMMAND... -- run an arbitrary command on the BURN
# node as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER under key-only auth. Pass-through: argv is
# handed to ssh as the remote command without shell wrapping (operator
# prepends `powershell -Command`, `bash -c`, etc. as the platform requires).
# Caller must have invoked bujb_resolve_investiture beforehand. Returns
# ssh's exit code.
bujb_privileged_ssh() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
    || buc_die "bujb_privileged_ssh: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"
  test $# -ge 1 \
    || buc_die "bujb_privileged_ssh: command required"

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUW Zipper - Colophon registry for BUK workbench dispatch

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${ZBUWZ_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZBUWZ_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Colophon registry initialization

zbuwz_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUWZ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "buwz already kindled"

  # Verify buz zipper is kindled (CLI furnish must kindle buz first)
  zbuz_sentinel

  # TabTarget subsystem (buut_cli.sh)
  local z_mod="buut_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_LIST_LAUNCHERS      "buw-tt-ll"  "${z_mod}" "buut_list_launchers"               ""  "List all registered launchers"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_BATCH_LOGGING       "buw-tt-cbl" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_batch_logging"      ""  "Create batch tabtarget with logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_BATCH_NOLOG         "buw-tt-cbn" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_batch_nolog"        ""  "Create batch tabtarget without logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_INTERACTIVE_LOGGING "buw-tt-cil" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_interactive_logging" ""  "Create interactive tabtarget with logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_INTERACTIVE_NOLOG   "buw-tt-cin" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_interactive_nolog"  ""  "Create interactive tabtarget without logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_LAUNCHER            "buw-tt-cl"  "${z_mod}" "buut_launcher"                     ""  "Create launcher script"

  # Config Regime subsystem (burc_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burc_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RC_VALIDATE "buw-rcv" "${z_mod}" "burc_validate"  ""  "Validate BURC regime"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RC_RENDER   "buw-rcr" "${z_mod}" "burc_render"    ""  "Render BURC regime"

  # Station Regime subsystem (burs_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burs_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RS_VALIDATE "buw-rsv" "${z_mod}" "burs_validate"  ""  "Validate BURS regime"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RS_RENDER   "buw-rsr" "${z_mod}" "burs_render"    ""  "Render BURS regime"

  # Environment Regime subsystem (bure_cli.sh)
  z_mod="bure_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RE_VALIDATE "buw-rev" "${z_mod}" "bure_validate"  ""  "Validate BURE regime"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RE_RENDER   "buw-rer" "${z_mod}" "bure_render"    ""  "Render BURE regime"

  # Node Regime subsystem (burn_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burn_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RN_VALIDATE "buw-rnv" "${z_mod}" "burn_validate" "param1" "Validate BURN profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RN_RENDER   "buw-rnr" "${z_mod}" "burn_render"   "param1" "Render BURN profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RN_LIST     "buw-rnl" "${z_mod}" "burn_list"     ""       "List BURN profiles"

  # Privileged Regime subsystem (burp_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burp_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RP_VALIDATE "buw-rpv" "${z_mod}" "burp_validate" "param1" "Validate BURP profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RP_RENDER   "buw-rpr" "${z_mod}" "burp_render"   "param1" "Render BURP profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RP_LIST     "buw-rpl" "${z_mod}" "burp_list"     ""       "List BURP profiles"

  # Qualification subsystem (buq_cli.sh)
  z_mod="buq_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_QUALIFY_SHELLCHECK "buw-qsc" "${z_mod}" "buq_shellcheck"  ""  "Run shellcheck on all tools"

  # Test fixtures (bux_cli.sh)
  z_mod="bux_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_DELAY "buw-xd" "${z_mod}" "bux_delay"  ""  "Sleep 20 seconds (timing fixture)"

  # Self-test (butt_testbench.sh)
  z_mod="butt_testbench.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_SELF_TEST "buw-st" "${z_mod}" "buw-st"  ""  "BUK self-test (kick-tires + bure-tweak)"

  # Handbook — jurisdiction (buhj_cli.sh)
  z_mod="buhj_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HJ0_TOP           "buw-hj0"   "${z_mod}" "buhj_top"                ""        "Jurisdiction handbook landing + admin SSH bootstrap"

  # Jurisdiction operational — workload ceremonies (bujb_cli.sh)
  z_mod="bujb_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_JW_KNOCK          "buw-jwk"   "${z_mod}" "bujb_knock"               "param1"  "Knock — probe workload SSH reachability"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_JW_COMMAND_FILE   "buw-jwc"   "${z_mod}" "bujb_command_file"        "param1"  "Run command file as workload, capture outputs"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_JW_INTERACTIVE    "buw-jws"   "${z_mod}" "bujb_interactive_session" "param1"  "Interactive SSH session as workload"

  # Jurisdiction operational — fenestrate (bujb_cli.sh)
  buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_FENESTRATE       "buw-jpF"   "${z_mod}" "bujb_fenestrate_command" "param1"  "Fenestrate — admin SSH trust + sshd_config harden (Windows)"

  # Jurisdiction operational — privileged SSH pass-through (bujb_cli.sh)
  buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_PRIVILEGED_SSH   "buw-jpS"   "${z_mod}" "bujb_privileged_ssh_command" "param1"  "Privileged SSH — run a command on a node as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER"

  # Jurisdiction operational — garrison ceremonies (bujb_cli.sh)
  buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_GARRISON_BASH    "buw-jpGb"  "${z_mod}" "bujb_garrison_bash"     "param1"  "Garrison workload (shell-letter b: native bash, Linux/Mac)"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_GARRISON_CYGWIN  "buw-jpGc"  "${z_mod}" "bujb_garrison_cygwin"   "param1"  "Garrison workload (shell-letter c: Cygwin bash, Windows)"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_GARRISON_WSL     "buw-jpGw"  "${z_mod}" "bujb_garrison_wsl"      "param1"  "Garrison workload (shell-letter w: WSL bash, Windows)"

  readonly ZBUWZ_KINDLED=1
}

######################################################################
# Healthcheck (validates all enrolled tabtargets exist on disk)

zbuwz_healthcheck() {
  zbuwz_sentinel
  buz_healthcheck
}

######################################################################
# Internal sentinel

zbuwz_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBUWZ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buwz not kindled - call zbuwz_kindle first"
}

# eof

lines: buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_PRIVILEGED_SSH   "buw-jpS"   "${z_mod}" "bujb_privileged_ssh_command" "param1"  "Privileged SSH — run a command on a node as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER"
+  # Jurisdiction operational — WSL install (bujb_cli.sh, Windows only)
+  buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_WSL_INSTALL      "buw-jpW"   "${z_mod}" "bujb_wsl_install_command"    "param1"  "WSL Install — provision canonical WSL distribution from Ubuntu-24.04 seed (Windows, idempotent)"
buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_GARRISON_BASH    "buw-jpGb"  "${z_mod}" "bujb_garrison_bash"     "param1"  "Garrison workload (shell-letter b: native bash, Linux/Mac)"
buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_GARRISON_CYGWIN  "buw-jpGc"  "${z_mod}" "bujb_garrison_cygwin"   "param1"  "Garrison workload (shell-letter c: Cygwin bash, Windows)"

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUJB CLI - Command line interface for the jurisdiction module
#
# Furnish loads BURC, BURS, BURP (per investiture folio), and the cross-
# referenced BURN profile, then kindles bujb. Command functions call
# bujb_resolve_investiture before doing any work; per-verb platform
# invariants are asserted inline at the command body.

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

######################################################################
# Command Functions

# bujb_resolve - diagnostic: load investiture and print resolved fields.
# No remote action; verifies regime + key-file health for a target.
bujb_resolve() {
  buc_doc_brief "Cross-validate an investiture and display the regime fields it resolves"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  bujb_resolve_investiture

  buc_step "Resolved investiture ${BUZ_FOLIO}:"
  buc_bare "  BURN_HOST               = ${BURN_HOST}"
  buc_bare "  BURN_PLATFORM           = ${BURN_PLATFORM}"
  buc_bare "  BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER    = ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}"
  buc_bare "  BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE= ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"
  buc_bare "  BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE  = ${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"
  buc_bare "  BURC_WORKLOAD_USER      = ${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
}

# bujb_knock - probe workload reachability (workload SSH + remote no-op).
bujb_knock() {
  buc_doc_brief "Probe workload SSH reachability for an investiture"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  bujb_resolve_investiture

  buc_step "Knocking ${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BURN_HOST} (${BUZ_FOLIO})"

  ssh -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
      -o ConnectTimeout=10                        \
      "${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BURN_HOST}" \
      true                                        \
    || buc_die "Knock failed for ${BUZ_FOLIO}"

  buc_step "Knock succeeded"
}

# bujb_command_file - stream a local command file's contents to the workload
# remote shell. Captures stdout/stderr/exit to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR. Per spec:
# returns 0 when dispatch + capture complete (independent of remote exit);
# non-zero on SSH-level failure (exit 255).
bujb_command_file() {
  buc_doc_brief "Execute a local command file as workload; capture stdout/stderr/exit"
  buc_doc_param "command_file" "Local file streamed as the remote command body"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  local z_command_file="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_command_file}"   || buc_usage_die
  test -f "${z_command_file}"   || buc_die "Command file not found: ${z_command_file}"
  test -r "${z_command_file}"   || buc_die "Command file not readable: ${z_command_file}"

  bujb_resolve_investiture

  buc_step "Streaming ${z_command_file} to ${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BURN_HOST}"
  buc_step "Output dir: ${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}"

  local z_exit=0
  ssh -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
      -o ConnectTimeout=10                        \
      "${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BURN_HOST}" \
      'bash -s'                                   \
      < "${z_command_file}"                       \
      > "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/stdout.log"           \
      2> "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/stderr.log"          \
    || z_exit=$?

  echo "${z_exit}" > "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/exitcode"

  test "${z_exit}" -ne 255 \
    || buc_die "SSH connection or authentication failed (exit 255). See ${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/stderr.log"

  buc_step "Remote exit code: ${z_exit}"
  buc_step "Capture complete"
}

# bujb_fenestrate - fenestrate ceremony (Windows OpenSSH only).
bujb_fenestrate_command() {
  buc_doc_brief "Fenestrate — admin SSH key trust + sshd_config harden (Windows)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_fenestrate
}

# bujb_privileged_ssh_command - run an arbitrary command as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER.
# Pass-through: argv joined into ssh's remote-command position; no shell wrapping.
bujb_privileged_ssh_command() {
  buc_doc_brief "Privileged SSH — run a command on a node as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER"
  buc_doc_param "command..." "Command tokens executed on the remote node (pass-through)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  test $# -ge 1            || buc_usage_die

  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_privileged_ssh "$@"
}

# bujb_garrison_bash - garrison ceremony, native bash workload shell (Linux/Mac).
bujb_garrison_bash() {
  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_linux/bubep_mac node (shell-letter b)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_garrison b
}

# bujb_garrison_cygwin - garrison ceremony, Cygwin bash workload shell (Windows).
bujb_garrison_cygwin() {
  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_windows node via Cygwin (shell-letter c)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_garrison c
}

# bujb_garrison_wsl - garrison ceremony, WSL bash workload shell (Windows).
bujb_garrison_wsl() {
  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_windows node via WSL (shell-letter w)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_garrison w
}

# bujb_interactive_session - hand control to ssh as workload.
bujb_interactive_session() {
  buc_doc_brief "Interactive SSH session as workload"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  bujb_resolve_investiture

  buc_step "Opening interactive session: ${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BURN_HOST} (${BUZ_FOLIO})"

  exec ssh -t                                     \
      -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
      "${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BURN_HOST}" \
      "bash -i"
}

######################################################################
# Furnish and Main

zbujb_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_CONFIG_DIR       " "BUK config directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burc_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burs_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bubc_constants.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burn_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burp_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bupr_PresentationRegime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buh_handbook.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buwz_zipper.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bujb_jurisdiction.sh"

  zbuv_kindle
  zburd_kindle
  zburd_enforce

  source "${BURD_REGIME_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURC: ${BURD_REGIME_FILE}"

  zburc_kindle
  zburc_enforce

  source "${BURD_STATION_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURS: ${BURD_STATION_FILE}"

  zburs_kindle
  zburs_enforce

  zbupr_kindle
  zbuz_kindle
  zbuwz_kindle
  zbujb_kindle

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  local -r z_burp_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/${BUBC_rbmu_users_subdir}/${BURS_USER}/${BUZ_FOLIO}/burp.env"
  test -f "${z_burp_file}" || buc_die "BURP profile not found: ${z_burp_file}"
  source "${z_burp_file}"  || buc_die "Failed to source BURP: ${z_burp_file}"
  zburp_kindle
  zburp_enforce

  # Cross-reference: investiture name (BUZ_FOLIO) must equal a registered investiture.
  # The 1:1 constraint is enforced by file presence — the BURN profile directory
  # name IS the investiture, and it must match BUZ_FOLIO exactly.
  local -r z_burn_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/${BUBC_rbmn_nodes_subdir}/${BUZ_FOLIO}/burn.env"
  test -f "${z_burn_file}" || buc_die "Investiture ${BUZ_FOLIO} has no matching investiture (BURN profile not found): ${z_burn_file}"
  source "${z_burn_file}"  || buc_die "Failed to source BURN: ${z_burn_file}"
  zburn_kindle
  zburn_enforce
}

######################################################################
# Public: WSL Install (provision canonical WSL distribution)

zbujb_wsl_install_assert_platform() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${BURN_PLATFORM}" = "bubep_windows" \
    || buc_die "wsl-install requires bubep_windows, got '${BURN_PLATFORM}'"
}

# bujb_wsl_install -- idempotently provision BUJB_wsl_distribution by purging
# any prior state, installing an Ubuntu-24.04 seed, exporting it to a .tar,
# importing under the canonical name, then unregistering the seed and removing
# the .tar. Caller must have invoked bujb_resolve_investiture beforehand.
# Each step propagates failure via zbujb_admin_powershell + || buc_die.
bujb_wsl_install() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
    || buc_die "bujb_wsl_install: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"

  zbujb_wsl_install_assert_platform

  local -r z_seed='Ubuntu-24.04'
  local -r z_install_dir='C:\WSL'
  local -r z_distro_dir="${z_install_dir}\\${BUJB_wsl_distribution}"
  local -r z_tar_path="${z_install_dir}\\${BUJB_wsl_distribution}.tar"

  buc_step "WSL Install: ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} on ${BURN_HOST} (seed: ${z_seed})"

  buc_step "  [1/6] Purge prior state (idempotent: unregister both, remove tar+dir)"
  local -r z_purge_body="if ((wsl.exe --list --quiet) -match '${BUJB_wsl_distribution}') { wsl.exe --unregister ${BUJB_wsl_distribution}; if (\$LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { exit \$LASTEXITCODE } }; if ((wsl.exe --list --quiet) -match '${z_seed}') { wsl.exe --unregister ${z_seed}; if (\$LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { exit \$LASTEXITCODE } }; if (Test-Path '${z_tar_path}') { Remove-Item -Force '${z_tar_path}' }; if (Test-Path '${z_distro_dir}') { Remove-Item -Recurse -Force '${z_distro_dir}' }"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "${z_purge_body}" \
    || buc_die "Purge step failed"

  buc_step "  [2/6] Ensure ${z_install_dir} directory"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path '${z_install_dir}' -Force | Out-Null" \
    || buc_die "Failed to create ${z_install_dir}"

  buc_step "  [3/6] Install ${z_seed} seed distribution"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --install --no-launch -d ${z_seed}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to install ${z_seed} seed"

  buc_step "  [4/6] Export ${z_seed} to ${z_tar_path}"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --export ${z_seed} '${z_tar_path}'" \
    || buc_die "Failed to export ${z_seed}"

  buc_step "  [5/6] Import ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} from ${z_tar_path}"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --import ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} '${z_distro_dir}' '${z_tar_path}'" \
    || buc_die "Failed to import ${BUJB_wsl_distribution}"

  buc_step "  [6/6] Cleanup: unregister ${z_seed} and remove .tar"
  local -r z_cleanup_body="wsl.exe --unregister ${z_seed}; if (\$LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { exit \$LASTEXITCODE }; if (Test-Path '${z_tar_path}') { Remove-Item -Force '${z_tar_path}' }"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "${z_cleanup_body}" \
    || buc_die "Cleanup step failed"

  buc_step "WSL Install succeeded"
}

######################################################################
# Public: Privileged SSH (thin admin-side pass-through)

content.text:
All wiring verified:

- **`tt/buw-jpW.WslInstall.sh`** created and executable; usage path correctly emits the no-folio diagnostic
- **`buwz_zipper.sh`** enrollment in place between PRIVILEGED_SSH and garrison block
- **`bujb_cli.sh`** wrapper `bujb_wsl_install_command` follows the fenestrate-style pattern
- **`bujb_jurisdiction.sh`** new public function `bujb_wsl_install` and platform-assert helper
- **Preflight diagnostic** reduced from 5 copy-paste lines to 2 (tabtarget + inspect)

**Implementation shape (six steps, all routed through `zbujb_admin_powershell`):**

```
[1/6] Purge prior state (idempotent: unregister both distros if present, remove .tar+dir if present)
[2/6] Ensure C:\WSL directory exists (New-Item -Force, idempotent)
[3/6] Install Ubuntu-24.04 seed (--install --no-launch -d)
[4/6] Export Ubuntu-24.04 to C:\WSL\rbtww-main.tar
[5/6] Import rbtww-main from the .tar
[6/6] Cleanup: unregister Ubuntu-24.04 + remove .tar
```

**Failure-stops-process discipline:**
- Each of the six SSH calls has `|| buc_die "step description"` on the bash side
- Helper enforces `$ErrorActionPreference='Stop'` + `$env:WSL_UTF8=1` + trailing `$LASTEXITCODE` check
- Multi-statement steps (1 and 6) include explicit intermediate `if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { exit $LASTEXITCODE }` after each native command, so intra-step failures propagate too

**Idempotency via delete-before-create:** Step 1 unconditionally tears down any prior `rbtww-main` and `Ubuntu-24.04` distros, plus the .tar and install dir. Steps 2–5 then build fresh state. Re-running on a half-completed prior run is safe.

I haven't run this against bujn-winpc — it does a real ~30+ second provisioning that mutates host state. Run `tt/buw-jpW.WslInstall.sh bujn-winpc` when ready, and I'll diagnose anything that breaks.

params.intent:
Introduce buw-jpW (WSL Install) tabtarget for idempotent provisioning of BUJB_wsl_distribution from an Ubuntu-24.04 seed. Replaces the five-line copy-paste workflow that the preflight diagnostic emitted with a single tabtarget invocation. Operator paste of the prior workflow had real UX failures: install isn't idempotent (ERROR_ALREADY_EXISTS on second run), export needs C:\WSL parent dir to exist (ERROR_PATH_NOT_FOUND), import requires the .tar from the missing export step.

New public function bujb_wsl_install in bujb_jurisdiction.sh runs six discrete steps via zbujb_admin_powershell, each with bash-side || buc_die and the helper's $ErrorActionPreference='Stop' / $env:WSL_UTF8=1 / trailing $LASTEXITCODE check:
  [1/6] Purge prior state (multi-statement: unregister both distros if present with intermediate $LASTEXITCODE checks, remove .tar+dir if present)
  [2/6] Ensure C:\WSL directory (New-Item -Force, idempotent)
  [3/6] Install Ubuntu-24.04 seed (--install --no-launch)
  [4/6] Export Ubuntu-24.04 to C:\WSL\${BUJB_wsl_distribution}.tar
  [5/6] Import ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} from the .tar
  [6/6] Cleanup: unregister Ubuntu-24.04 + remove .tar

Delete-before-create idempotency model: step 1 tears down any prior state of both the canonical and seed distros plus their files, so re-running on a half-completed prior run is safe. Failure-stops-process: each step's || buc_die on bash side; multi-statement steps include explicit if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { exit $LASTEXITCODE } after each native command so intra-step failures propagate.

zbujb_wsl_install_assert_platform mirrors zbujb_fenestrate_assert_platform pattern (BURN_PLATFORM == bubep_windows). bujb_wsl_install_command in bujb_cli.sh wraps with buc_doc_brief and bujb_resolve_investiture per the fenestrate/garrison pattern. Zipper enrollment BUWZ_JP_WSL_INSTALL placed between privileged-SSH and garrison block.

Preflight diagnostic in zbujb_garrison_w_preflight reduced from 5-line copy-paste sequence to 2 lines: tt/buw-jpW.WslInstall.sh ${BUZ_FOLIO} (the install) plus the inspect line. bash -n green on all three modified bash modules. No-folio path verified: tt/buw-jpW.WslInstall.sh (no args) emits the standard burp_die_no_folio diagnostic listing available investitures. Live execution against bujn-winpc deferred to operator (mutates host state, ~30+ seconds for download).
